body.title: CC inbox producer: Igor posts ticket-trip + escalation events
body.result: cc_inbox_bridge + 2 pe_chain hooks (DESIGN_PROPOSAL high/response-expected, block normal/response-expected). scope_guard covered transitively. 6 tests. 940439db.
body.description:
Add producer hooks so Igor can post to the CC inbox. Three initial producers: (1) cc_queue ticket-trip handler when a go-live-when condition fires, (2) pe_chain ESCALATE when consult-exhausted, (3) scope_guard block on HIGH-inertia file. Each producer imports cc_inbox.append() and posts with appropriate kind + urgency. Non-fatal — producer failures logged but don't break the triggering path.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/cc_inbox_bridge.py (new — wraps lab.claudecode.cc_inbox for igor.cognition.* callers), wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (ESCALATE-consult-exhausted hook), wild_igor/igor/tools/scope_guard.py (HIGH-inertia block hook), lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (trip-condition-fire hook)
**Design rules:** docs-live-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not
**Scope boundary:** IN — three producer hook sites + bridge module. OUT — other subsystems (they can hook in as follow-ons), changing trigger semantics.
**Test plan:** unit tests verify each producer-site calls cc_inbox.append() with correct kind/ticket_id/summary; producer-failure is non-fatal (mock append() raising; verify caller path continues).
